paper_title: Scalable Training of Mixture Models via Coresets
core_contribution: The paper proposes a coreset construction algorithm for Gaussian Mixture Models that creates a weighted subset of O(dk^3/ε^2) data points that preserves the log-likelihood approximation quality. The method uses adaptive sampling based on distances to an initial rough clustering to construct coresets whose size is independent of the original dataset size.
algorithm_steps: Step 1: Initialize empty set B and copy dataset D to D'
Step 2: While |D'| > 10dk ln(1/δ), sample S of 10dk ln(1/δ) points uniformly from D', remove closest |D'|/2 points to S from D', add S to B
Step 3: Add remaining points in D' to B to get final approximation set B
Step 4: Partition original dataset D into clusters based on closest points in B
Step 5: Compute sampling probabilities m(x) for each point based on distance to B and cluster membership
Step 6: Sample coreset C of size O(d²k|B|² ln(1/δ)/ε²) points according to probabilities m(x)
Step 7: Assign weights ω(x') to sampled points based on inverse sampling probabilities
key_equations: Theorem 3.1: Coreset approximation guarantee (1-ε)Φ(D|θ) ≤ Φ(C|θ) ≤ Φ(D|θ)(1+ε)
Lemma 3.2: Reduction of log-likelihood to Euclidean distance computation ln P(x|θ) = f(x)(Ψ(θ)) + Z(θ)
Sampling probability: m(x) = l/(5|D_b|) + dist(x,B)²/Σ_{x'∈D} dist(x',B)²

key_assumptions.assumption: Covariance matrices have eigenvalues bounded in [ε, 1/ε] (ε-semi-spherical Gaussians)
key_assumptions.why_needed: Prevents degenerate solutions and ensures well-conditioned covariance matrices for the geometric reduction to work
key_assumptions.violation_scenario: When data has very flat or very concentrated clusters with extreme aspect ratios

key_assumptions.assumption: Data is generated i.i.d. from the mixture model
key_assumptions.why_needed: Required for the log-likelihood decomposition and probabilistic guarantees to hold
key_assumptions.violation_scenario: When data has temporal dependencies or spatial correlations

key_assumptions.assumption: Number of mixture components k is known and fixed
key_assumptions.why_needed: The coreset size depends polynomially on k and the algorithm needs k for the initial clustering
key_assumptions.violation_scenario: When the true number of components is unknown or varies across different regions of the data

baselines_compared: Standard EM algorithm on full dataset
baseline_limitations_identified: Uniform sampling can perform arbitrarily poorly when mixture components have very different weights, potentially missing rare but important components entirely
proposed_improvement: Adaptive sampling based on distances to an initial rough clustering ensures representative points from all clusters are included with appropriate probabilities
condition_where_baseline_wins: When the dataset is small enough to fit in memory and computational resources are abundant, running EM on the full dataset would be more accurate than using a coreset approximation
datasets_used: Earthquake detection accelerometer data from mobile phones
suitable_toy_datasets: 2D synthetic Gaussian mixtures with varying weights
toy_dataset_justification: These datasets are small enough to verify coreset quality against full dataset results while having clear cluster structure that matches the Gaussian mixture assumption
reproducibility_notes: Algorithm is clearly specified but implementation details for the geometric reduction and handling of edge cases in the adaptive sampling are not fully detailed

key_components_for_ablation.role_in_method: Creates the initial rough clustering B that guides the final coreset sampling
key_components_for_ablation.expected_effect_of_removal: Would likely require much larger coresets to achieve same approximation quality, especially for unbalanced mixtures

key_components_for_ablation.role_in_method: Ensures points far from the initial clustering B are sampled with higher probability
key_components_for_ablation.expected_effect_of_removal: Would reduce approximation quality as outliers and boundary points would be underrepresented

key_components_for_ablation.component: Iterative point removal in initial clustering
key_components_for_ablation.role_in_method: Progressively refines the approximation set B by removing points closest to already sampled points
key_components_for_ablation.expected_effect_of_removal: Would result in less representative initial clustering, potentially missing important regions

known_failure_modes.scenario: Mixture components with very different scales or highly elongated clusters
known_failure_modes.reason: Violates the ε-semi-spherical assumption about bounded eigenvalues
known_failure_modes.related_assumption: Covariance matrices have eigenvalues bounded in [ε, 1/ε]

known_failure_modes.scenario: Very high-dimensional data with sparse structure
known_failure_modes.reason: Coreset size grows polynomially with dimension d, making it impractical
known_failure_modes.related_assumption: Implicit assumption that dimension d is moderate

known_failure_modes.scenario: Data with significant outliers or noise
known_failure_modes.reason: Distance-based sampling may oversample outliers, and the i.i.d. assumption is violated
known_failure_modes.related_assumption: Data is generated i.i.d. from the mixture model
